title: Proving your identity with the GOV.UK One Login app
body: "You can use the GOV.UK One Login app to prove your identity when you sign in to a government service with GOV.UK One Login.   To use the app, you must start on the government service you are trying to access. You’ll be guided to the app when it’s time to use it.   The app will check that:   your photo ID is real you are a real person you are the same person as in your photo ID   If you prefer not to use the app, you can prove your identity in another way.   What you’ll need    You’ll need one of the following types of photo ID:   UK passport non-UK passport with a biometric chip UK photocard driving licence UK biometric residence permit (BRP) UK biometric residence card (also called a BRC) UK Frontier Worker permit (FWP)    You can use an expired BRP, BRC or FWP up to 18 months after its expiry date.   You’ll also need a smartphone with a working camera.   If you’re using an iPhone, it must be:    an iPhone 8 or newer running iOS 16.7 or higher   For an Android phone (for example, Samsung or Google Pixel), it must be running Android 10 or higher.  Downloading the app   You’ll be told when to download the app.  If you need help to download the app to your phone, there is advice on:   downloading an app to an iPhone downloading an app to an Android phone   Signing in to the app  After downloading the app, you’ll need to sign in with your GOV.UK One Login details. This will link the GOV.UK website to your app so you can continue proving your identity.  If you’re having trouble signing in to the app, read guidance on how to change your GOV.UK One Login sign in details.  Choosing your photo ID   You’ll need to choose which photo ID you’ll use to prove your identity. Make sure you have your original photo ID with you. You cannot use a photocopy or digital copy.  Once you’ve confirmed the photo ID you’ll use,  you cannot go back and use a different one.  If you need to change to a different type of photo ID, you’ll need to go back to the service you were trying to use and start proving your identity again.   Scanning your photo ID  If you’re using a passport or a BRP, BRC or FWP   If you’re using a passport, make sure it has a biometric chip. To check if your passport has a biometric chip, look for the rectangular biometric chip symbol on the front cover.   You’ll be asked to:   Take a photo of your photo ID Scan the biometric chip in your photo ID using your phone. Scan your face using your phone.   If the biometric scan did not work, it can be because:   your phone was not touching your photo ID your phone had a case or accessory on it - try removing these and scanning your photo ID again internet connection has been lost briefly the phone was...
